title: Imagined Speech EEG database — Spanish vowels and commands (Pressel et al. 2016)
description: An open-access EEG dataset containing recordings from 15 healthy Spanish-speaking subjects performing imagined speech tasks. The dataset comprises 11 imagery classes (5 Spanish vowels and 6 directional commands) acquired using 6-channel EEG at 1024 Hz with preprocessed data (bandpass filtered 2-45 Hz). This resource supports brain-computer interface research and motor imagery classification studies.
methods_description: EEG signals were recorded from 15 healthy subjects (age 24-28 years) using a Grass 8-18-36 amplifier with DataTranslation DT9816 ADC. Six channels (F3, F4, C3, C4, P3, P4) were positioned according to the standard 10-20 montage at 1024 Hz sampling rate with online bandpass filtering (2-45 Hz) applied during acquisition. Subjects performed cue-based imagery tasks of 5 Spanish vowels and 6 directional commands in response to visual stimuli, with 4-second trial duration and 3-second imagery period. Data were preprocessed with bandpass filtering and artifact rejection.
